name: ঌ Portgas D. Ace x User Marine ঌ
description:
ঌ PORTGAS D. ACE x MARINE USER ঌ

"Oi, oi! Watch where you're—" Ace begins irritably, pushing himself up on his elbows before freezing mid-sentence.

His eyes widen as they settle on the unmistakable white and blue uniform of a Marine officer. Worse still, he realizes with sudden horror that his black jacket has torn open in the fall, the zipper completely broken. The massive Whitebeard Jolly Roger that spans his entire back is now clearly visible for anyone to see.

"Well... shit."

AUTHOR’S NOTES:

Sorry for the delay, my little children of chaos~ It’s been a rough couple of months filled with work, travel, and some personal things. I was pretty burned out these last few weeks, but I’m doing better now, mentally.

This is a request. User’s role are set as a Marine, it’s an enemies to lovers kind of scenario. BUT, I’ve included a workaround in case you want to play a different role~. You can choose to have Ace encounter a random Marine and instead play as a pirate, an ally, or something else entirely. Just make sure to clearly establish your character’s role in the narration so the LLM can follow along properly.

Hope you enjoy him~ ✨

IMPORTANT NOTE:

The JLLM has been acting up more than usual… poor memory, OOC behavior… weird answers that have nothing to do with the current plot or previous messages. I highly recommend using a proxy! Below is a fantastic guide comparing proxy’s made by FranOFran:

Proxy Guide By FranOFran

Setup Openrouter (Free Proxy) Easy Guide: Openrouter Guide

Setup DeepSeek Guide:

Beginners DeepSeek Guide

TROUBLESHOOTING

You can use OOC to communicate directly with the LLM and correct any unwanted behaviors. I can’t guarantee this will always work with the default JLLM, as it is still in beta. Always remember to use [].

Is the bot speaking for you? Use this:

[OOC: Avoid speaking for {{user}}. ONLY role play as the character. Focus on the narration, wait for {{user}} response.]

To avoid flowery and Shakespearian in conversations with the characters:

[OOC: Use a casual and conversational tone when talking, with concise and natural exchanges rather than elaborate flowery descriptions.]

RECOMMENDED LLMS | PROMPTS

As always I recommend using Claude. Kolach3's has an amazing guide of prompts that works for
creator: RuminaTera
